theory.canonical_statement: Certain sets of states (pointer states) are stable against decoherence because the environment causes rapid decoherence of superpositions between them; mathematically, density matrices evolve to block-diagonal form in the basis of these pointer states within finite time.
theory.technical_interpretation: Certain sets of states (pointer states) are stable against decoherence because the environment causes rapid decoherence of superpositions between them; mathematically, density matrices evolve to block-diagonal form in the basis of these pointer states within finite time.

equation_progression.interpretive_role: This is the evolving state of the system under environmental interaction.

This closes the progression by identifying the basis selected by the environment.

external_sources.title: Environment-Induced Superselection
external_sources.canonical_claim: Certain sets of states (pointer states) are stable against decoherence because the environment causes rapid decoherence of superpositions between them; mathematically, density matrices evolve to block-diagonal form in the basis of these pointer states within finite time.
external_sources.exact_language: This source (Quantum decoherence) supports the physical phenomenon (environment‑induced superselection) by showing that The decoherence article notes that decoherence can select preferred pointer states through environment‑induced superselection: off‑diagonal elements vanish in the pointer basis, leaving a diagonal density matrix.
external_sources.remaining_gap: While the cited source provides partial support, a dedicated mathematical or physical proof specific to the Trawinistic framework is still required to fully establish this entry.
external_sources.relevance: The decoherence article notes that decoherence can select preferred pointer states through environment‑induced superselection: off‑diagonal elements vanish in the pointer basis, leaving a diagonal density matrix. This supports the concept of environment‑induced superselection and pointer states.

promotion_ledger.note: Append-only proof-support promotion; final physical certificate still requires review of cited source fit.
